title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Model Repair Bay
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested numerical Python notebook and module and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.
Choose visual encodings, scales, labels, and uncertainty displays that support the question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reproducible Computation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pin inputs and environments and separate exploration from rerunnable analysis.
